corePrinciple.insight: Components should be like good waiters: If the kitchen sends out bad food (broken data source), don't drop the plate and run away (crash). Show the customer the problem clearly (error indicator) so they know to talk to the chef (fix their data source).
corePrinciple.goldenRule: Never crash. Always communicate. Show users exactly what went wrong and where to fix it.

architecture.rule: Defensive validation belongs at the BOUNDARY LAYER - where external data enters your component hierarchy

layers.analogy: Kitchen utensils (a knife doesn't check if food is fresh)
Cooking stations (a grill doesn't validate ingredients)
The chef (inspects ingredients before cooking, rejects bad ones)

reasons: Domain components are the API boundary - where external data enters
Only domain components know the context - what 'valid status' means
Primitives should stay simple - no validation bloat
Error messages need context - domain layer knows the business rules

enumValidation.name: isValidEnumValue

stringValidation.name: isValidString

numberValidation.name: isValidNumber

dateValidation.name: isValidDate
dateValidation.catches: Invalid Date objects, unparseable date strings

safeCompare.name: safeCompare
safeCompare.use: Safe sort comparison - invalid values sort last

designRequirements.container: Inline display with visual distinction (border, background tint)
designRequirements.tooltip: Explains what went wrong and where to fix it

informationToDisplay: Where to fix it (hint: 'Check your data source/adapter')

good: Invalid status: 'OPENN' - Check your data adapter

gracefulDegradationLevels.behavior: Display error indicator in place of bad data

expectations: Component should NOT crash - no unhandled exceptions
Component SHOULD show error indicators - clear visual feedback
Component SHOULD remain interactive - other features still work
Error state SHOULD be accessible - screen readers can announce issues
testing.sortFilterSafety: When implementing sort/filter functionality, handle invalid values - invalid values should sort last

checklist: All external data fields have validation
Invalid data shows error indicators, not crashes

quickReference.validationDecision: Domain/boundary component? → YES: Validate → NO: Trust props
